Using selectFrom(CHAT) fetches all columns from the CHAT table, which may retrieve unnecessary data and reduce performance. Consider explicitly selecting only the required columns using select(CHAT.CHAT_ID, ...).from(CHAT).

Using selectFrom(LINK) fetches all columns from the LINK table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Consider explicitly selecting only the required columns using select(LINK.ID, LINK.URL, ...).from(LINK).

Code fragment: return dslContext.resultQuery("select * from LINK where EXTRACT(SECOND FROM (now() -last_check_time )) > 30")
Explanation: Using 'select *' fetches all columns from the LINK table, which retrieves unnecessary data and reduces maintainability. Consider explicitly listing only the required columns in the SELECT clause.

Using selectFrom(LINKS_SOF) fetches all columns from the LINKS_SOF table, which can retrieve unnecessary data. Consider explicitly selecting only the required columns using select(...).from(LINKS_SOF).
